name: Antivirus Software Discovery via WMI
description: This prompt detects the use of Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI) to query for antivirus software information. Attackers often perform this reconnaissance activity to identify security products and tailor their attacks to evade detection. The prompt is designed to help analysts investigate the use of `wmic.exe` with specific command-line arguments related to 'SecurityCenter2' and 'AntiVirusProduct'.

prompts.prompt: Investigate the security event where the process `{process}` executed the command `{command}`. The event occurred at `{timestamp}` on the host `{hostname}`. The command includes arguments to query for 'SecurityCenter2' and 'AntiVirusProduct', which is a common method for attackers to discover antivirus software. Analyze the parent process (`{parent_process}`) and the user (`{user}`) who initiated this command. Determine if this activity is part of a legitimate administrative task or a potential reconnaissance attempt. Provide a summary of your findings and any recommended actions.

parameters.name: process
parameters.description: The name of the process that was executed (should be wmic.exe).

parameters.name: command
parameters.description: The full command line of the wmic.exe process, including the query for antivirus products.

parameters.name: hostname
parameters.description: The hostname where the event was detected.

parameters.name: parent_process
parameters.description: The parent process that initiated the wmic.exe command.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user account under which the command was executed.
